'''''Star Wars Tales'' 4''' is the fourth issue of the ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' series of [[Star Wars comic books|comics]]. It was published on [[June 28]], [[2000]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Kevin Rubio, creator of the hilarious short film ''TROOPS'', lends his bit of craziness to this month's ''Tales''. Lovern Kindzierski and fan-favorite Doug Wheatley (Aliens: Apocalypse) team up to spin a yarn. And Kilian Plunkett's comical "Sandblasted " follows up on the acclaimed adventures of Jabba the Hutt's nefarious swoop thugs, Big Gizz and Spiker, who encounter a dark trooper in the ruins of Tatooine's Podracing arena. The trooper is malfunctioning, but its weapons systems are still fully functional! Mayhem ensues.''

==Contents==
{{Quote|Dear Editor. I think you need more lightsaber fights in STAR WARS TALES. Sincerely, Max Denning (age 3)|Dave Land, from a reader's letter|<ref name="Tales 4">''Star Wars Tales'' 4</ref>}}
''Star Wars Tales'' editor [[David Land|Dave Land]] introduces the issue and presents a letter from three-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old reader [[Max Denning]], requesting more [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] in the series. In response, Land arranges a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] between [[K'Kruhk]], from the [[Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'']] series, and that series' artist [[Ramón F. Bachs]]. K'kruhk [[Death/Legends|strikes down]] the unprepared Bachs, while Land, unphased, encourages readers to continue with the issue and to send further feedback.
*"[[Moment of Doubt]]"
*"[[A Death Star Is Born]]"
*"[[Spare Parts (comic story)|Spare Parts]]"
*"[[Sand Blasted]]"
